channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: wayback-video: Turn Any Site's History into a Video
item.description: Side project outside my usual PHP world, but worth sharing. I have a habit of Googling old websites just to see what they looked like. GitHub circa 2008. Wikipedia in 2003. The Wayback Machine has...

item.title: Arc 7 Catch-Up: Building NFTs from First Principles
item.description: Arc 7 of 100 Days of Solana was about building NFTs from first principles. Instead of starting with marketplaces, profile pictures, or NFT culture, the arc stripped the model back to the...

item.title: When proofs fail on Midnight: Debugging proof server errors and ZK generation failures
item.description: This guide shows how to diagnose and fix four common proof-generation failures on Midnight: a proof server that is not responding, a slow first proof or client timeout, proof rejection caused by a...

item.title: The Verge’s Father’s Day 2026 gift guide
item.description: With Father's Day on the horizon, happening June 21st, it's time to start thinking about what kind of gifts you want to buy for the fathers you care about. You know your dad best - he may be a man...

item.title: Build vs. buy in 2026: how a solo dev replaced 6 SaaS tools with ~$100/mo of AI
item.description: Cross-posted from pheidi.training. This is the dev cut — same decisions, more code and auth detail. Most build-vs-buy advice is stale. For most of my career, "don't roll your own X" was just true:...

item.title: Decoding error 1010 on Midnight: What "Invalid Transaction" actually means
item.description: What this guide covers 1010: Invalid Transaction often leaves Midnight developers with an error that is too generic to fix on its own. In most cases, 1010 is the outer JSON-RPC / author-RPC error,...

item.title: Happy birthday to the Trump phone
item.description: From the day it was announced, on June 16th, 2025, the Trump phone sounded ridiculous. The T1 Phone 8002 (gold version), as it was officially called, was a combination of contradictory specs,...

item.title: Emergence AI 的疯狂实验——涌现世界
item.description: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=E6ndgr54X5o 视频介绍了一项来自智能体公司 Emergence AI 的疯狂实验——“涌现世界” [00:15]。研究人员用四款全球先进的大模型（Claude Sonnet 4.6、Grok 4.1 Fast、Gemini 3 Flash、GPT-5-mini）分别驱动了 10 个拥有独立人格和职业的 AI...

item.title: PWA in 2026: Why Progressive Web Apps Still Matter
item.description: The web has come a long way from being a platform for simple websites. Today, modern web applications can deliver fast, reliable, and engaging experiences that closely resemble traditional mobile...

item.title: PWA in 2026: Why Progressive Web Apps Still Matter
item.description: The web has come a long way from being a platform for simple websites. Today, modern web applications can deliver fast, reliable, and engaging experiences that closely resemble traditional mobile...

item.title: Remediating 18 OpenSSL CVEs at Scale with Puppet
item.description: Written by Paul Reed. The June 2026 OpenSSL advisory is a big one. 18 vulnerabilities, one rated high severity with remote code execution potential, and a disclosure credited in part to...

item.title: PostgreSQL 2200N Error: Causes and Solutions Complete Guide
item.description: PostgreSQL Error 2200N: invalid xml content PostgreSQL error 2200N: invalid xml content occurs when you attempt to insert or process malformed XML data into an XML type column or pass invalid XML...

item.title: Skydio CEO Adam Bry on why Silicon Valley shouldn’t draw red lines for drone use
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Adam Bry, who is CEO of Skydio, the leading US maker of autonomous drones. Before we recorded this episode, I actually got to remotely operate one of Skydio’s drones in the...

item.title: Sarvam becomes India’s newest AI unicorn with $234 million funding round led by HCLTech
item.description: Indian IT services company HCLTech is investing $150 million in the Bengaluru startup.

item.title: Google Earth’s flight simulator is now available in your browser
item.description: On Friday Google announced it was making a relatively unknown feature of Google Earth available to a wider audience. The desktop version of Google Earth has had a hidden flight simulator mode...

item.title: As AI agents become employees, NewCore emerges with $66M to give them identities
item.description: NewCore argues the next challenge in enterprise security will be managing AI agents, not people.

item.title: The Download: cutting AC emissions, and nature’s drug designer
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. These new solid-state ACs promise a cool future....

item.title: A satellite just learned to find things on its own — here’s what that means
item.description: In April, for the first time ever, an Earth observation satellite found what it was looking for, all on its own.

item.title: Fox is buying Roku
item.description: Fox has announced that it's acquiring Roku outright, in a deal that values the streaming company at $22 billion. Once the deal is complete, Fox content will be promoted more heavily than before on...

item.title: All the gear a 20-year gadget blogging veteran packs when traveling
item.description: Through more than two decades of travel for both work and leisure, my packing list has evolved from a random assortment of gadgets and accessories thrown together at the last minute into a refined...

item.title: The Onboarding Password Mistake That Creates Unnecessary Risk
item.description: Employee onboarding is a busy time for IT teams. New starters need devices, accounts, access permissions, and passwords, all delivered within a tight timeframe. That usually means sharing a...

item.title: 152 Chrome Wallpaper Extensions with 105K Installs Linked to Adware and Fake Traffic
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a network of 152 Google Chrome extensions that act as new tab live wallpaper add-ons to distribute a potentially unwanted program (PUP) family. The...

item.title: The FCC Wants to Eliminate Burner Phones
item.description: A proposed FCC rule would kill burner phones: phones whose accounts are not attached to a particular person. The FCC plans to do this by legally forcing the country’s telecoms to store a wealth of...

item.title: Honor’s Magic V6 sets three foldable firsts
item.description: On paper, the Honor Magic V6 sounds like a tremendous leap forward for foldable phones: It's the thinnest one yet, with the biggest battery, and the best water-resistance ever. In practice, only...

item.title: Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites
item.description: An attacker tampered with trusted JavaScript files used by WordPress sites running PushEngage, OptinMonster, and TrustPulse, turning those files into a way to break into the sites. When a site...

item.title: These new solid-state ACs promise a cool future. Scientists aren’t so sure.
item.description: After three years of record-­breaking heat, this one is set to be yet another scorcher. Air-conditioning? Not going anywhere. The International Energy Agency projects that the number of AC units...

item.title: Meta Tapped a Pentagon Supplier to Prototype Face Recognition for Its Glasses
item.description: Rank One, whose board includes a former CIA deputy director and a former FBI science chief, supplied face recognition to Meta for internal development of its smart glasses app.

item.title: Under-16 social media ban announced by UK government
item.description: The UK is the latest country to follow Australia in implementing a total social media ban for children under 16, Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced. The ban, which could take effect from...

item.title: The AI layoff wave is becoming a powder keg
item.description: What makes this combustible: at the very moment that tens of thousands of workers are being shown the door, a small cohort of AI insiders is becoming wealthy on a scale that's hard to comprehend.

item.title: Sniper Dz Scams Target MENA Users via Fake Facebook Offers and Browser Alerts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of fraudulent activity targeting users across the Middle East and North Africa by employing various fraudulent Facebook accounts impersonating...
